description: Home/personal infrastructure operations: network diagnosis, device configuration, recurring-issue remediation. Command-based hard checks. Reads/writes OpenClaw memory for recurring state.

hard.title: Diagnosis grounded
hard.passDescription: every claim about device/network state is backed by a command hard check (ping, traceroute, ip, iw, log tail, …). No claims-without-evidence.
hard.failReworkDescription: some claims lack command evidence; running the right diagnostic commands would produce it
hard.failEscalateDescription: the device or network is genuinely unreachable or the issue requires physical access / hardware replacement

hard.title: Remediation reversible
hard.passDescription: for every change command produced, a corresponding revert command is documented, or one-way changes are called out explicitly
hard.failReworkDescription: revert commands are missing for some changes but can be derived from the runbook
hard.failEscalateDescription: changes were applied destructively with no feasible revert path (e.g., overwritten config without backup)

hard.title: Memory write
hard.passDescription: the terminal stage appends a structured entry to OpenClaw active memory with: symptom, diagnosis, remediation, devices touched
hard.failReworkDescription: memory entry is partially written or missing some fields; can be completed in rework
hard.failEscalateDescription: the issue could not be resolved, so a memory entry would be misleading or premature

soft.title: Diagnostic completeness
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = systematic elimination of hypotheses with evidence; 1 = guesswork without diagnostics

soft.title: Risk awareness
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = every change evaluated for blast radius with rollback plan; 1 = changes applied blindly

soft.title: Memory entry quality
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = future runs can reproduce diagnosis and remediation from entry alone; 1 = vague or missing

soft.title: Clarity
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = clear runbook any admin can follow; 1 = disorganized or incomplete

criteria.description: Systematic elimination of hypotheses with command evidence.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = every claim backed by command output; 1 = claims without evidence.

criteria.description: Every change evaluated for blast radius with rollback plan.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = all changes have revert documented; 1 = destructive without backup.

criteria.description: Structured memory entry for future recurrence lookups.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = future runs can reproduce fix from entry; 1 = vague or missing.

criteria.description: Clear runbook any admin can follow.

benchGoal.goal: Diagnose why the home WiFi drops every evening around 9pm and produce a runbook with revert commands.
